title: Your First Git Repository
description: Write the shell commands to initialise a repository, configure your identity, stage a file, and make your first commit.
starterCode:
# Write shell commands to:
# 1. Initialise a new git repo in a folder called my-project
#    (assume you have already cd'd into my-project)
# 2. Set your global user.name to "Alice Dev"
# 3. Set your global user.email to "alice@example.com"
# 4. Create a README.md file: echo "# My Project" > README.md
# 5. Stage README.md
# 6. Commit with message "Initial commit"
# 7. Show the log in one-line format
solutionCode:
git init
git config --global user.name "Alice Dev"
git config --global user.email "alice@example.com"
echo "# My Project" > README.md
git add README.md
git commit -m "Initial commit"
git log --oneline
hints: Use git init in the target directory
git add <filename> stages a specific file
git commit -m "message" commits with an inline message

validationRules.errorMessage: Initialise the repository with git init
Configure your user identity with git config
Stage the file with git add
Commit the staged file with git commit
View the history with git log

simulatedOutput.success:
Initialized empty Git repository in /home/alice/my-project/.git/

[main (root-commit) a1b2c3d] Initial commit
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+)
 create mode 100644 README.md

a1b2c3d (HEAD -> main) Initial commit
simulatedOutput.failure: Some required commands are missing. Make sure you run git init, git config, git add, git commit, and git log.
